channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Akani Simbine: South African Sprint Star Builds Legacy Through Consistency and Longevity
item.description: Akani Simbine, the South African 100m sprint sensation, first broke the 10-second barrier in 2015 and has since done so 50 times officially, showcasing remarkable consistency and longevity in a sport ...

item.title: Aso Rock Staff Claims He Was Misled by Colleagues in Alleged Coup Case
item.description: One Zekari Umoru, wey dey work for Julius Berger Nigeria Plc inside Maintenance Department for Presidential Villa, Abuja, don tell court sey him dey involved in alleged coup plan against President Bol...

item.title: PNP enforces three-strike accountability for unpaid fuel incidents
item.description: The Philippine National Police (PNP) has introduced a three-strike policy to hold police commanders accountable for failing to prevent or respond effectively to 'gas-and-run' cases. This move follows ...

item.title: SHO in Shikarpur killed after police firing due to mistaken identity
item.description: In a tragic incident early Tuesday morning, SHO Ali Hassan of Lakhi Ghulam Shah police station was killed in Shikarpur, Sindh, when a policeman opened fire on his car, reportedly mistaking him for a s...

item.title: NCLAT Dismisses Vedanta’s Appeal Against Adani’s Acquisition of Jaypee Assets
item.description: The National Company Law Appellate Tribunal (NCLAT) on Monday rejected two petitions filed by Vedanta Limited challenging the selection of Adani Enterprises to acquire debt-laden Jaiprakash Associates...

item.title: President Ruto Calls for Stronger Kenya-Tanzania Cooperation and Regional Integration
item.description: President William Ruto delivered a special address to Tanzania's Parliament during his state visit on 5th May 2026, highlighting the long-standing historical ties between Kenya and Tanzania. He praise...

item.title: Analysis of the Liberal Government's Choice of Louise Arbour as Governor General
item.description: The article critiques the Liberal government's appointment of Louise Arbour as Canada's next governor general. Chris Selley argues that the choice is controversial, largely because Arbour, a former Su...

item.title: Man claims self-defence in airport officer assault case
item.description: A man appearing in court over allegations of assaulting a police officer at an airport has stated that he acted in self-defence, believing both he and his brother were under threat at the time of the ...

item.title: Why combining strength training and cardio delivers the best long-term health benefits
item.description: Strength training has become increasingly popular due to its clear benefits for maintaining muscle mass, supporting healthy ageing, and improving physical independence. Experts note that it can also p...

item.title: Empowering Women Scientists is Essential for Africa’s Health Progress
item.description: Women in Africa are making critical contributions to health research, yet they remain underrepresented in leadership positions, which is limiting the continent’s scientific and health progress. While ...

item.title: Top tennis players threaten French Open boycott over prize money fairness
item.description: Tennis world is seeing some serious tension ahead of this year’s French Open. Women’s world number one, Aryna Sabalenka, has warned that players might boycott the tournament if the prize money is not ...

item.title: SIU Investigates Inflated Generator Costs and Irregular Procurement in Ditsobotla Municipality
item.description: The Special Investigating Unit (SIU) has referred a R25 million procurement case involving generators for the Boikhutsong pump stations in Ditsobotla Local Municipality to the Special Tribunal. The pr...

item.title: African Governments Urged to Reform Telecom Taxes to Boost Digital Access
item.description: Daddy Mukadi, the Chair of GSMA Africa Policy Group and Chief Regulatory Officer at Airtel Africa, is calling on African governments to urgently reform tax policies in the telecommunications sector. S...

item.title: IMF warns economic effects of Iran conflict may take months to stabilise
item.description: The IMF Managing Director, Kristalina Georgieva, has warned that the economic consequences of the ongoing conflict involving Iran are expected to take at least three to four months to ease, even if th...

item.title: Infrasound in Buildings Linked to Stress and Irritability
item.description: Recent research suggests that the eerie or unsettling feelings often reported in old buildings may be linked to infrasound, a type of sound vibration below the threshold of human hearing. Infrasound c...

item.title: Githurai Photographer and Family Live in Car After Theft of Equipment
item.description: Francis Kutieto, a photographer based in Githurai, Nairobi, is currently living in his car with his wife Felister and their two-and-a-half-year-old daughter after a series of misfortunes left them hom...

item.title: Investigation suggests misogynistic culture contributed to oversight in Northern Ireland showjumper case
item.description: The recent investigation into the tragic murder of a showjumper in Northern Ireland has highlighted serious concerns regarding institutional and societal attitudes towards women. Authorities have sugg...

item.title: Large Wildfire Spreads Rapidly on Vancouver Island
item.description: A significant wildfire on Vancouver Island has escalated rapidly, prompting heightened concern among local authorities and residents. Emergency services are actively battling the blaze, which has spre...

item.title: BOA Niger records stronger Q1 2026 profit amid cost cuts and loan clean-up
item.description: BOA Niger don show clear sign say dem dey recover from the tough period wey dem face for 2025, as their first quarter 2026 result show strong improvement for profit and efficiency. The bank record net...

item.title: Blake Lively and Justin Baldoni Settle Lengthy Legal Dispute Over Film Production
item.description: Blake Lively and Justin Baldoni, co-stars of the 2024 film adaptation of Colleen Hoover's bestseller 'It Ends With Us', have finally resolved their long-running legal dispute. What began as an alleged...
